advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Glycemic index (GI) and glycemic load (GL) of foods are important but must be contextualized within overall meal composition — fat, fiber, and protein all lower postprandial glucose response. Low-carbohydrate diets are the most effective dietary intervention for reducing HbA1c in type-2 diabetes. Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data reveals highly individual glycemic responses to the same foods across different people.
research_findings: Low-carbohydrate diets reduce HbA1c by 0.5–1.5% in type-2 diabetic patients across multiple randomized controlled trials. The PREDIMED trial demonstrated that a Mediterranean diet reduces type-2 diabetes incidence by 52% versus a low-fat control diet. Vinegar and cinnamon consumption modestly improve post-meal insulin sensitivity through distinct mechanisms.
current_focus_areas: Integrating CGM-derived personal glycemic response data into dietary recommendations; developing carbohydrate-sequencing protocols (eating fiber and protein before carbohydrates) to blunt glucose spikes; building risk stratification tools for prediabetes dietary intervention.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 58%.
